original_abstract: This study frames attack detection within the smart grid as a statistical learning challenge, addressing various attack scenarios across both batch and online measurement settings. We propose a detection framework that utilizes machine learning algorithms to classify measurements as either secure or compromised. By leveraging prior system knowledge, this approach effectively overcomes data constraints imposed by the sparse structure of grid networks. To model the detection process, we employ a suite of supervised and semi-supervised learning algorithms integrated with decision- and feature-level fusion. Furthermore, we analyze the relationship between the statistical and geometric properties of attack vectors to demonstrate how learning-based methods can identify "unobservable" attacks that typically bypass conventional checks. Extensive testing on IEEE standard systems indicates that the proposed machine learning framework consistently outperforms traditional state-vector estimation methods in terms of detection accuracy and reliability.
humanized_abstract: In this paper, the attack detection in smart grid is turned into a statistical learning problem. In the batch and online measurement modes, the attacks under different attack scenarios are dealt with by designing a detection method based on machine learning. The detected measurements are classified into safe and attacked based on the machine learning model, and the prior knowledge of the system is used to offset the adverse effect of the limited number of samples caused by the sparse structure of the power grid. Finally, by using various supervised and semi-supervised learning algorithms and combining decision-level fusion and feature-level fusion, and analyzing the relationship between the statistical characteristics and the geometric characteristics of the attack vectors, the practicability and superiority of the machine learning method are verified by simulating and comparing the IEEE standard system with the state-vector estimation method.
